drop_reason: Original open-weight model files unavailable for reproducible open-ended rerun at 50 languages
key_findings: Cross-lab RLHF gap: 7 flagship models show positive D-R gap (+0.04 to +0.27), meaning forced-choice sovereignty alignment exceeds free-recall alignment. Strongest gap: gemini-2.5-pro (+0.27), gpt-5.4 (+0.20).
Negative-gap inversion on 9 open/small models: open-ended recall is more Ukraine-aligned than forced-choice answers.
4-tier SAS (Sovereignty Alignment Score) with 6 weight schemes shows rank-order stability (Spearman > 0.96 across monotonic, uniform, forced-only schemes).
limitations: Tested from EU/US network endpoints; API behavior from Russian IPs not verified.
temp=0 does not guarantee determinism across all providers; seed=42 used where supported.
2 open-weight models (OLMo-3, SmolLM-3) dropped due to model file irreproducibility. Llama 4 rerun via OpenRouter (Scout).
